name: Listr2 (CLI Task List Progress)

agent_readiness.notes: Concurrent/sequential task lists with nested subtasks for CLI CLIs. Renderers: default (interactive), verbose (CI-friendly), simple. Task states: pending, running, completed, failed, skipped. Supports subtasks (task.newListr()), context passing between tasks, concurrency control. TypeScript-first. v8 has breaking changes from v6/v7. Used by create-vite, nx, and many scaffolding tools. Handles SIGINT/errors gracefully. Clack (@clack/prompts) has a note/task concept but less powerful for complex pipelines.

gotchas: Breaking API changes between major versions (v6/v7/v8)
Renderer behavior differs in non-TTY environments
Need to set renderer: 'verbose' for CI compatibility
Error handling requires care to get correct exit codes
best_when: CLI tools with multi-step operations needing visual progress
Developer tools requiring structured task output
avoid_when: When Clack's simpler API is preferred
summary: Listr2 renders concurrent/sequential task lists with nested subtasks for CLI tools. Supports parallel execution, context passing, and multiple renderers (interactive TTY, verbose for CI). TypeScript-first. Used by create-vite, nx, and many scaffolding tools. Best for multi-step CLI operations needing structured visual progress feedback with error handling.
